test_quality: The EditMode suite is directionally good for this milestone: assertions are specific, the tests are focused on one concern at a time, and they cover the main contract surfaces introduced by EPIC-00 and EPIC-01. However, test execution evidence is missing, Unity coverage was not measured, and the current review already identified missing Unity .meta files, so test adequacy cannot be accepted as complete yet.
missing_coverage: No executed Unity EditMode test run is available, so the authored tests are unverified in the actual Unity runner.
No measured line, branch, or function coverage is available; the implementation summary records coverage as null.
No RED/GREEN evidence exists for the TDD cycle because both phases are still marked unverified in the implementation artifact.
No test coverage addresses the missing Unity .meta files, which are a real asset-integrity risk for the new Assets/Core, Data, LLM, UI, and Tests trees.
The path-resolution helpers that depend on Application.dataPath were reviewed statically but not validated in a live Unity editor test run.
